name: FoundationDB
description: Apple's open-source distributed, transactional key-value store with unique layered architecture

license.comment: Open sourced by Apple in April 2018 under Apache 2.0

hosting-options.comment: No official managed service; self-hosted only

initial-release.comment: Version 1.0 released August 20, 2013. Alpha program started January 2012.

data-model.comment: Ordered key-value store with lexicographical key ordering. Higher-level data models (document, relational, graph) implemented via layers.

topology.comment: Decoupled architecture with heterogeneous roles: Cluster Controller (singleton), Master, Sequencer, Proxies, Resolvers, LogServers, StorageServers

sharding.comment: Data Distributor automatically assigns key ranges to storage servers and ensures even distribution

replication-factor.comment: Triple replication recommended for 5+ machines. Three_datacenter mode replicates 6 times across 3 DCs.

multi-region.comment: Supports up to two regions with automatic failover. Multi-DC mode replicates across three datacenters.

cap-position.comment: Prioritizes consistency with strict serializability. May reject writes during partitions.

consistency-model.comment: Strict serializability - transactions appear to occur sequentially

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID transactions spanning multiple machines

transaction-scope.comment: Transactions can span any keys across the entire cluster

isolation-levels.comment: Serializable isolation with optimistic concurrency control (OCC)

distributed-transactions.comment: Cross-node transaction coordination via Sequencer, Proxies, and Resolvers

query-language.comment: No query language - pure key-value API. SQL and other query capabilities provided via layers (e.g., Record Layer)

sql-compatibility.comment: Core database has no SQL. SQL available via layers like FoundationDB Record Layer.

secondary-indexes.comment: Core key-value store has no built-in secondary indexes. Implemented via layers.

joins.comment: No join support in core database. Available via layers.

aggregations.comment: No built-in aggregations. Implemented at application level or via layers.

max-cluster-size.comment: Official benchmark used 384-process cluster (24 machines x 16 cores). Apple runs much larger clusters in production for iCloud.

write-throughput.comment: Benchmark on commodity hardware with SATA SSDs

read-latency-p99.comment: Single-core transactional reads from fully durable database. Commit latency ~2-3ms.

auto-scaling.comment: Adding nodes is easy but manual. No automatic scale-out/scale-in.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Data Distributor automatically rebalances data across storage servers. Ratekeeper monitors load.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: True moment-in-time snapshot backup and restore capability

backup-strategy.comment: Integrated backup system with scheduled snapshots to remote filesystem

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official operator developed by Apple's FoundationDB SRE team

dotnet-driver.comment: Community binding by Doxense, available on NuGet

c-cpp-driver.comment: Official C bindings; all other bindings wrap the C client

orm-support.comment: Key-value store - no traditional ORM support. Layers provide higher-level abstractions.

open-source.comment: Apache 2.0 license, open sourced by Apple in April 2018

cost-estimate-small.comment: Self-hosted only; infrastructure costs depend on cloud provider

change-data-capture.comment: No built-in CDC. Watch API exists for single keys only, not ranges.

watch-subscribe.comment: Watch API for single keys. Watches fire once and must be re-registered. No range watches.

triggers.comment: No server-side triggers in core database

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Could not retrieve Stack Overflow tag count; community primarily uses FoundationDB forums
